ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general green tea beverage class (FOODON_03301099); no specific passionfruit-flavoured green tea with reduced sugar exists. Closest_parent match used; passionfruit flavouring and reduced-sugar customisation are not captured in the ontology.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP covers ready-to-drink green tea. Passionfruit flavouring and reduced-sugar customisation are not represented; closest_parent match is the closest defensible anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept for passionfruit-flavoured green tea beverage with reduced sugar. SNOMED CT not applied.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard cup of passionfruit green tea (less sugar, no topping) is approximately 350 ml / 350 g, as typically served at Singaporean bubble tea and specialty tea outlets.

nutrient_source.title: Passionfruit Green Tea (Less Sugar) — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for passionfruit green tea (less sugar, no topping), cr_id D08010082. All seven reported nutrients (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre) are taken verbatim from the HPB record at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and micronutrient fields not reported by HPB are set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for passionfruit green tea (less sugar, no topping) in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 55 (low) by analogy with sugar-sweetened tea beverages and fruit-flavoured tea drinks reported in Atkinson et al. (2008) and the University of Sydney GI Database. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (55) × carbohydrate per serving (g) / 100 = 55 × (6.12 g/100g × 350 g serving / 100) = 55 × 21.42 / 100 ≈ 11.8, rounded to 11.7. This is an analogous estimate; direct measurement is recommended for clinical use.

hypertension.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage classification (6.12 g sugar/100 g) independently raises blood pressure risk; sodium is low at 2 mg/100 g
hypertension.guidance: Although sodium is negligible (2 mg/100 g), the 6.12 g/100 g sugar content classifies this as a sugar-sweetened beverage, which independently elevates blood pressure risk per AHA 2018 guidelines; individuals with hypertension should request a genuinely zero-sugar version or choose unsweetened green tea.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 6.12 g sugar/100 g and negligible dietary fibre (0.17 g/100 g); liquid sugar raises postprandial glucose rapidly
type2_diabetes.guidance: This beverage contains 6.12 g sugar/100 g with virtually no fibre (0.17 g/100 g), classifying it as a sugar-sweetened beverage per ADA guidelines; individuals with type 2 diabetes should avoid this drink and choose an unsweetened (sugar = 0 g) version to prevent rapid postprandial glucose excursions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages is associated with increased cardiovascular risk; fat content is low (0.28 g/100 g) and sodium is negligible
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The low fat (0.28 g/100 g) and negligible sodium (2 mg/100 g) profile is favourable for cardiovascular disease, but the 6.12 g/100 g sugar content means regular consumption contributes to added sugar intake linked to dyslipidaemia and cardiovascular risk; limit to occasional consumption or substitute with unsweetened green tea.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Sugar at 6.12 g/100 g with negligible fibre promotes pro-inflammatory mediators; no meaningful anti-inflammatory nutrients present
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The 6.12 g/100 g sugar content with negligible fibre (0.17 g/100 g) may promote inflammatory mediators relevant to rheumatoid arthritis; unsweetened green tea would provide EGCG anti-inflammatory benefits without the pro-inflammatory sugar load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Passionfruit Green Tea (Less Sugar, No Topping) is a freshly brewed green tea beverage flavoured with passionfruit, prepared with a reduced quantity of added sugar and served without any customisable topping such as tapioca pearls or jelly. It is a popular specialty tea drink widely available at Singaporean bubble tea and specialty beverage outlets. The drink has a light, mildly sweet, and slightly tangy flavour profile from the passionfruit flavouring, with the grassy, vegetal notes of green tea as the base. The texture is thin and liquid, similar to a lightly sweetened iced tea. Per 100 g, it provides 27 kcal, 0.17 g protein, 0.28 g fat, 6.12 g carbohydrate (of which 6.12 g is sugar), 0.17 g dietary fibre, and 2 mg sodium. Despite the 'less sugar' designation, the beverage retains 6.12 g sugar per 100 g, which classifies it as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) under ADA and AHA guidelines. A standard 350 ml serving delivers approximately 21.4 g of sugar and an estimated glycemic load of 11.7. Clinically, this drink is not recommended for individuals with type 2 diabetes or hypertension due to its SSB classification. The low sodium and low fat content are positive attributes for cardiovascular health, but the sugar content remains a concern for regular consumption. The removal of toppings reduces total caloric and carbohydrate load compared to the standard version but does not resolve the SSB classification. Unsweetened green tea would be the preferred alternative for health-conscious consumers.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre) are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database at confidence 0.95 for cr_id D08010082. The source is an approved primary regional source for Singapore foods. GI is an analogous estimate (medium confidence for GI only) as no direct measurement exists for this specific beverage variant.
notes: Fields set to -1 (data not available from HPB source): saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g. Reason: HPB Singapore Foodo database entry D08010082 does not report detailed fatty acid fractions or micronutrient data for this specialty beverage. No supplementary approved source (USDA FoodData Central, ICMR-NIN, FAO/INFOODS SEA) contains an exact match for this specific passionfruit green tea less sugar formulation. GI is an analogous estimate based on sugar-sweetened tea beverages from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2008); direct GI measurement for this product is not available. The 'less sugar' designation reduces sugar relative to the standard version but the beverage remains an SSB at 6.12 g sugar/100 g. Protein_g (0.17) and fat_g (0.28) are non-zero values from HPB; no zero-macro justification required as no core macro is zero.
